title: Total RNA and DNA Extraction from Microalgae Samples
author: Yingyu Hu

steps.title: Freeze-dry samples
steps.instruction: Freeze-dry samples. Store at -80 °C . Freeze-drying should be as short as possible to reduce sample degradation. The exact duration of freeze-drying depends on size of filter, quantity of sample and the size of container. FreeZone® 2.5 L Benchtop Freeze Dryers (Labconco®, 700202000)

steps.title: Turn on UV light in biosafety cabinet for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Turn on UV light in biosafety cabinet for 00:15:00

steps.title: Clean working surface with decontamination solution
steps.instruction: Clean working surface with decontamination solution.

steps.title: Prepare Tris buffer 5 mM pH 8
steps.instruction: Prepare Tris buffer 5 mM pH 8.0

steps.title: Pour 1 M pH 8
steps.instruction: Pour 1 M pH 8.0 Tris into an RNase free 15 mL Falcon tube Tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane hydrochloride 1M pH 8.0 RNase free Fisher Scientific Catalog #AAJ60080AK (CAS: 1185-53-1) Falcon® Centrifuge Tubes (Polypropylene, Sterile, 15 mL, Corning®, 352096)

steps.title: Directly add 2
steps.instruction: Directly add 2.5 mL 1 M pH 8.0 Tris into 500 mL RNase free water in its original package. UltraPure™ DNase/RNase-Free Distilled Water Thermofisher Catalog #10977023 BT Barrier Pipet Tips (Pre-Sterile, Neptune®, BT1250, BT100, BT10)

steps.title: RNA primary standard solution ( 200 ug/ml )
steps.instruction: RNA primary standard solution ( 200 ug/ml )

steps.title: In the original package, the frozen E
steps.instruction: In the original package, the frozen E. Coli Total RNA is of 1 mg/mL, in which total RNA is 200 ug. E. coli Total RNA Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #AM7940 https://assets.thermofisher.com/TFS-Assets/LSG/manuals/sp_7940.pdf

steps.title: Uncap the original package of E
steps.instruction: Uncap the original package of E. Coli Total RNA and directly add 800 µL Tris buffer ( 5 mM , pH 8.0 ) . Cap the package and invert for a thorough mix. Be aware of the package. If there is a conical bottom vial insert, add 400 ul Tris buffer first, cap the package, invert for a thorough mix. Transfer the solution to a 2 mL RNase free tube. Add another 400 ul Tris buffer, cap the package, invert for a thorough mix. Transfer and combine the solutions together in the 2 mL tube.

steps.title: Aliquot 30 uL by stepper with...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 30 uL by stepper with sterile tip to 600 uL RNase free microtubes. Store at -80 °C Finnpipette Stepper Pipette (Thermo Scientific™, 4540000) Finntip stepper pipette tips (500 ul (sterile), Thermo Scientific, Thermo Scientific™ 9404173)

steps.title: DNA primary standard solution (≈ 500 ug/ml )
steps.instruction: DNA primary standard solution (≈ 500 ug/ml )

steps.title: Uncap the original package of Deoxyribonucleic...
steps.instruction: Uncap the original package of Deoxyribonucleic acid from calf thymus and add 2 mL Tris buffer ( 5 mM , pH 8.0 ). Deoxyribonucleic acid from calf thymus Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #D4522-1MG https://www.sigmaaldrich.com/content/dam/sigma-aldrich/docs/Sigma/Product_Information_Sheet/d4522pis.pdf

steps.title: Cap the package
steps.instruction: Cap the package. Do not vortex or sonicate.

steps.title: Keep the solution in the fridge...
steps.instruction: Keep the solution in the fridge overnight to completely solubilize the DNA. Gentle reversion is recommended.

steps.title: Aliquot 10 uL by stepper with...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 10 uL by stepper with sterile tip to 600 uL RNase free microtubes. Store at -20 °C Finntip stepper pipette tips (500 ul (sterile), Thermo Scientific, Thermo Scientific™ 9404173)

steps.title: RNase primary stock solution ( 10 mg/ml )
steps.instruction: RNase primary stock solution ( 10 mg/ml )

steps.title: Uncap the original package of Ribonuclease...
steps.instruction: Uncap the original package of Ribonuclease A from bovin pancreas and add 5 mL Tris buffer ( 5 mM , pH 8.0 ). Cap the package and invert for a thorough mix. Ribonuclease A from bovine pancreas Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #R6513-50MG

steps.title: Aliquot 30 uL by stepper with...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 30 uL by stepper with sterile tip to 600 uL RNase free microtubes. Store at -20 °C Finntip stepper pipette tips (500 ul (sterile), Thermo Scientific, Thermo Scientific™ 9404173) Finntip™ Stepper Pipette Tips (500 ul (Sterile), Thermo Scientific, 21-377-149)

steps.title: DNase primary stock solution ( 5 mg/ml = 10,000 U/mL)
steps.instruction: DNase primary stock solution ( 5 mg/ml = 10,000 U/mL)

steps.title: Uncap the original package of Deoxyribonuclease1...
steps.instruction: Uncap the original package of Deoxyribonuclease1 and add 1 mL Tris buffer ( 5 mM , pH 8.0 ) . Cap the package and invert for a thorough mix. DEOXYRIBONUCLEASE1 RNase and Protease Free Bioshop Catalog # DRB002.10

steps.title: Use reverse pipetting to precisely aliquot...
steps.instruction: Use reverse pipetting to precisely aliquot 60 uL into 5 mL RNase free tube. Store at -20 °C . The 5 mL tube will be used as WS-A in the assay directly. One package of DNase can be used for 16 assays.

steps.title: Turn on UV light in biosafety cabinet for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Turn on UV light in biosafety cabinet for 00:15:00

steps.title: Clean working surface with decontamination solution
steps.instruction: Clean working surface with decontamination solution.

steps.title: Prepare falcon tubes and tube rack...
steps.instruction: Prepare falcon tubes and tube rack in biosafety cabinet Volume of the tube (mL) Content in the tube 1 5 0.5 M EDTA 2 5 20% sarcosine 3 50 5 mM Tris 4 15 or 50 1% STEB Falcon® Centrifuge Tubes (Polypropylene, Sterile, 15 mL, Corning®, 352096) Falcon® Centrifuge Tubes (Polypropylene, Sterile, 50 mL, Corning®, 352070)

steps.title: Prepare STEB ( 1 % )...
steps.instruction: Prepare STEB ( 1 % ) Use the following formula to determine the total volume of 1% STEB required: # samples X (500 ul) + (500 ul) = total volume of 1% STEB required

steps.title: Pour sarcosine ( 20 % )...
steps.instruction: Pour sarcosine ( 20 % ) into an RNase free 5 mL falcon tube. N-Lauroylsarosine sodium salt solution (20% RNase/DNase free) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #L744-50mL

steps.title: Pour EDTA ( 0
steps.instruction: Pour EDTA ( 0.5 M ) into an RNase free 5 mL falcon tube. EDTA buffer (0.5M DNase/RNase free) Bioshop Catalog #EDT333.100

steps.title: Pour Tris buffer ( 5 mM , pH 8
steps.instruction: Pour Tris buffer ( 5 mM , pH 8.0 ) into an RNase free 50 mL falcon tube.

steps.title: Mix the following ingredients to obtain...
steps.instruction: Mix the following ingredients to obtain STEB ( 1 % ): sarcosine ( 20 % ): 500 µL EDTA ( 0.5 M ): 10 µL Tris buffer ( 5 mM , pH 8.0 ): 9 mL + 490 µL

steps.title: Prepare ice bath
steps.instruction: Prepare ice bath

steps.title: Remove freeze-dried samples from -80ºC freezer...
steps.instruction: Remove freeze-dried samples from -80ºC freezer and place them On ice .

steps.title: Add 500 µL Tris buffer (...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL Tris buffer ( 5 mM , pH 8.0 ) and 500 µL STEB ( 1 % ) to the bead tube. Place tubes On ice . Lysing tube matrix D (2 mL, MP biomedical, 116913500) Use 2 mL bead tube for both 25 mm and 47 mm size filter.

steps.title: Rinse forceps by 70 % volume ethanol and air dry
steps.instruction: Rinse forceps by 70 % volume ethanol and air dry. Filter forceps (blunt end, stainless steel, Millipore, XX6200006P)

steps.title: Transfer sample/blank filter into the bead...
steps.instruction: Transfer sample/blank filter into the bead tube by using clean forceps. Prior to placing sample filter into the bead tube: (1) For filters folded into half-strip, unfold once to return to a strip. (2) For filters folded into quarter-circles, unfold once to return to a half-circle shape, then fold once along the dimension to form a strip. (2) For filters haphazardly into a compact mass, carefully unfold with two tweezers (avoiding losing biomass), fold once into a half-cricle shape, then fold once more along the dimension to form a strip

steps.title: Invert the tube then put back On ice
steps.instruction: Invert the tube then put back On ice .

steps.title: Disrupt samples on the bead mill at 6
steps.instruction: Disrupt samples on the bead mill at 6.5 m/s. Fastprep-24 5G™ Sample Preparation Instrument (MP Biomedicals, 116005500)

steps.title: Keep tubes On ice
steps.instruction: Keep tubes On ice . Check the label on each tube, restore the label if it fades.

steps.title: Disrupt samples on the bead mill at 6
steps.instruction: Disrupt samples on the bead mill at 6.5 m/s.

steps.title: Keep tubes On ice
steps.instruction: Keep tubes On ice . Check the label on each tube, restore the label if it fades.

steps.title: Disrupt samples on the bead mill at 6
steps.instruction: Disrupt samples on the bead mill at 6.5 m/s

steps.title: Keep tubes On ice
steps.instruction: Keep tubes On ice . Check the label on each tube, restore the label if it fades.

steps.title: Disrupt samples on the bead mill at 6
steps.instruction: Disrupt samples on the bead mill at 6.5 m/s.

steps.title: Continuously shake homogenate in a multi-head...
steps.instruction: Continuously shake homogenate in a multi-head vortex at the highest speed for 01:00:00 Room temperature Votex mixer should be able to remain stable on the bench under this vortex speed.

steps.title: In the biosafety cabinet, transfer supernatant for the assay
steps.instruction: In the biosafety cabinet, transfer supernatant for the assay.

steps.title: Store both boxes of samples at -80 °C
steps.instruction: Store both boxes of samples at -80 °C .

steps.title: Prepare ice bath
steps.instruction: Prepare ice bath.

steps.title: Turn on UV light in biosafety cabinet for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Turn on UV light in biosafety cabinet for 00:15:00

steps.title: Clean working surface with decontamination solution
steps.instruction: Clean working surface with decontamination solution.

steps.title: Prepare falcon tubes, microtubes and tube...
steps.instruction: Prepare falcon tubes, microtubes and tube racks in biosafety cabinet Number of tubes Type of tubes Contents 1 4 5 mL falcon tubes 1 M MgCl2 2 1 M CaCl2 3 Working solution B (WS-B) 4 Working solution C (WS-C) 5 1 50 mL falcon tube 5 mM Tris buffer 6 1 15 mL falcon tubes 0.05% STEB 7 6 2 mL RNase free tubes RNase working solution 8 RNA tertiary standard solution 9 DNA tertiary standard solution 10 900 mM MgCl2 11 900 mM CaCl2 12 Sybr green working solution (SG-II WS) 13 24 2 mL RNase free tubes RNA standard solutions for RNA standard curves 14 DNA standard solutions for DNA standard curves 15 3XN (N = Number of samples) 2 mL RNase free tubes Diluted samples 16 4 Microtube racks Tubes of 2 mL in Set 1 17 Tubes of 2 mL in Set A 18 Tubes of 2 mL in Set B 19 Tubes of 2 mL in Set C 20 1 Tube racks Falcon tubes Screw-Cap Centrifuge Tube (5 mL, VWR, 10002-738)

steps.title: Organize and label the tubes as...
steps.instruction: Organize and label the tubes as shown below, log sample numbers into the tube rack layout. Set 1: This tube rack holds sample extract (100 uL) to be further diluted. Set A, B and C: In microtube rack, label 2 mL tubes for RNA (marked in pink), DNA (marked in blue) standard solutions and samples (marked in yellow) Set A is for working solution A (WS-A) treatment, i.e. treated with DNase Set B is for working solution B (WS-B) treatment, i.e. treated with RNase Set C is for working solution A (WS-A) and C (WS-C) treatment, i.e. treated with DNase and RNase

steps.title: Label tubes for reagents as following
steps.instruction: Label tubes for reagents as following. Follow the sheet, add Tris buffer ( 5 mM , pH 8.0 ) to the reagent tubes: Content 5 mM Tris (uL) 1 SG-II WS 1000+250 2 WS-B 2X1000+820 3 WS-C 2X1000+940 4 RNase 380 5 900 mM MgCl2 40 6 900 mM CaCl2 40 7 RNA tertiary 690X2 8 DNA tertiary 960 9 0.05% STEB 9X1000 + 500

steps.title: Thaw Sybr green II at room...
steps.instruction: Thaw Sybr green II at room temperature SYBR™ Green II RNA Gel Stain, 10,000X concentrate in DMSO Thermo Fisher Catalog #S7564

steps.title: Add Tris buffer ( 5 mM , pH 8
steps.instruction: Add Tris buffer ( 5 mM , pH 8.0 ) to each tube in Set A, B and C . The unit of volume is uL. Avoid changing the dilution for samples in this step. The total volume required for samples in Set A, B and C is already 750 ul.

steps.title: Prepare STEB ( 0
steps.instruction: Prepare STEB ( 0.05 % ) Add 500 µL STEB ( 1 % ) to 0.05% STEB tube, and invert to mix.

steps.title: Add 250 µL STEB ( 0
steps.instruction: Add 250 µL STEB ( 0.05 % ) to RNA and DNA standards in Set A, B and C by reverse pipetting.

steps.title: Place sample extract, RNase and DNase...
steps.instruction: Place sample extract, RNase and DNase primary stock solutions, RNA and DNA primary standard solutions On ice .

steps.title: Turn on shaker/incubator and set temperature to 37 °C
steps.instruction: Turn on shaker/incubator and set temperature to 37 °C . SHAKING INCUBATOR (71L, Corning® LSE™, 6753)

steps.title: Prepare 900 mM MgCl 2
steps.instruction: Prepare 900 mM MgCl 2

steps.title: Pour 1 M MgCl 2 solution...
steps.instruction: Pour 1 M MgCl 2 solution into 5 mL RNase free Falcon tube Magnesium chloride solution Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #63069-100ML

steps.title: Transfer 360 µL 1 M MgCl...
steps.instruction: Transfer 360 µL 1 M MgCl 2 solution into 900 mM MgCl 2 tube

steps.title: Add 60 µL 900 mM MgCl 2 to WS-B
steps.instruction: Add 60 µL 900 mM MgCl 2 to WS-B

steps.title: Prepare 900 mM CaCl 2
steps.instruction: Prepare 900 mM CaCl 2

steps.title: Pour 1 M CaCl 2 solution...
steps.instruction: Pour 1 M CaCl 2 solution into 5 mL RNase free Falcon tube Calcium chloride solution Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #21115-100ML

steps.title: Transfer 360 µL 1 M CaCl...
steps.instruction: Transfer 360 µL 1 M CaCl 2 solution into 900 mM CaCl 2 tube

steps.title: Add 60 µL 900 mM CaCl 2 to WS-B
steps.instruction: Add 60 µL 900 mM CaCl 2 to WS-B

steps.title: Prepare SG-II WS
steps.instruction: Prepare SG-II WS

steps.title: Centrifuge one tube of SG-II concentrate...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge one tube of SG-II concentrate at Room temperature 13000 rpm, °C, 5 minutes to deposit DMSO.

steps.title: Wrap SG-II WS tube with foil,...
steps.instruction: Wrap SG-II WS tube with foil, transfer supernatant of SYBR Green II 10,000X concentrate to SG-II WS tube in biosafety cabinet ( 8.75 ul per 1.25 mL Tris) Any step involving SYBR Green II should be operated in dark room or at least dim light. Prepare Sybr green II WS at this step to allow enough time for stabilization.

steps.title: Lunch break
steps.instruction: Lunch break!

steps.title: Prepare RNase working solution 0
steps.instruction: Prepare RNase working solution 0.5 mg/ml Add 20 µL RNase primary stock solution ( 10 mg/ml ) to RNase tube

steps.title: Thoroughly mix RNase tube and then transfer 60 µL 0
steps.instruction: Thoroughly mix RNase tube and then transfer 60 µL 0.5 mg/ml RNase working solution to WS-B. Keep WS-B On ice .

steps.title: Thoroughly mix RNase tube and then transfer 60 µL 0
steps.instruction: Thoroughly mix RNase tube and then transfer 60 µL 0.5 mg/ml RNase working solution to WS-C. Keep WS-C On ice .

steps.title: Add 60 µL 900 mM CaCl 2 to WS-A
steps.instruction: Add 60 µL 900 mM CaCl 2 to WS-A. Keep WS-A On ice .

steps.title: Prepare DNA secondary standard solution 25 ug/ml
steps.instruction: Prepare DNA secondary standard solution 25 ug/ml

steps.title: Use reverse pipetting
steps.instruction: Use reverse pipetting: load 50 µL WS-A to tubes in Set A .

steps.title: Use reverse pipetting
steps.instruction: Use reverse pipetting: load 50 µL WS-A to tubes Set C .

steps.title: Use reverse pipetting
steps.instruction: Use reverse pipetting: load 50 µL WS-B to tubes in Set B .

steps.title: Use reverse pipetting
steps.instruction: Use reverse pipetting: load 50 µL WS-C to tubes in Set C .

steps.title: Organize sample extracts (i
steps.instruction: Organize sample extracts (i.e., the 100 uL aliquot) into Set 1 . Forward pipetting, add 900 uL 5 mM Tris buffer into each tube.

steps.title: Thoroughly mix sample prior to transferring
steps.instruction: Thoroughly mix sample prior to transferring. Follow the layout below to add diluted samples, RNA tertiary and DNA tertiary into Set A, B and C. The unit of volume is uL. Forward pipetting: (1) To avoid enzyme cross-contamination: Renew tip between sets when dispensing the same sample or standard tertiary solution (2) Aspire up and down for a complete dispense and thorough mix

steps.title: Invert each tube for thorough mixing,...
steps.instruction: Invert each tube for thorough mixing, then organize the tubes in a 96-well microtube rack following the same order as the microplates are loaded.

steps.title: After incubation, Invert each tube for...
steps.instruction: After incubation, Invert each tube for thorough mixing, then place them into the freezer for 2 min to stop the reaction.

steps.title: Check absorbance of SG-II WS:
steps.instruction: Check absorbance of SG-II WS:

steps.title: In a transparent microplate, load (1)...
steps.instruction: In a transparent microplate, load (1) 200 uL Tris buffer as blank (2) 10 uL SG-II WS and 190 uL Tris buffer

steps.title: Read absorbance at 480 nm, the...
steps.instruction: Read absorbance at 480 nm, the value after subtracted by blank shall be no higher than 0.21

steps.title: Remove samples out of the freezer...
steps.instruction: Remove samples out of the freezer and allow to reach Room temperature before loading the microplate. Since fluorescence decreases with increasing temperature, with percentage changes depending on the fluorophore (Bashford, 1987), the SG-II WS must be kept dark at RT (22ºC) and the samples must be equilibrated at RT (c. 2 min).

steps.title: Adhere black film on the top of a microplate lid
steps.instruction: Adhere black film on the top of a microplate lid. Black Vinyl Films for Fluorescence and Photoprotection (VWR, 89087-692) Microplate Lids (Polystyrene, Greiner Bio-One, 07000288)

steps.title: Load 10 µL SG-II WS to...
steps.instruction: Load 10 µL SG-II WS to each well by either 0.5 mL stepper or 10 uL pipet. Cover the plate with the black-film lid. 96-Well Black Microplates (Polystyrene, Greiner Bio-One, 655076)

steps.title: Reverse pipetting
steps.instruction: Reverse pipetting: load 190 µL working samples to the microplate. Wipe or dab the liquid drop on the outside of the tip, avoid wiping the tip open before dispensing the liquid.

steps.title: Shake black film covered microplate at...
steps.instruction: Shake black film covered microplate at Room temperature for 01:30:00 The fluorescence value and the ratio between RNA and DNA stabilize within 90 minutes, contrary to the 10 minutes reported in the original paper.

steps.title: Setup microplate reader
steps.instruction: Setup microplate reader: Plate: Greiner F bottom chimney well PP 96 well; Shake: Continuous 5s at 600 rpm Fluorescence bandwidth: 12 nm Exciation: 490 nm Emission: 520 nm Varioskan LUX Multimode Microplate Reader (Thermo Fisher, VL0L00D0) Bandwidth at 12 nm gives more consistent results compared to 5 nm bandwidth.

steps.title: Read fluorescence and export data to excel sheet
steps.instruction: Read fluorescence and export data to excel sheet.

steps.title: In the fume hood, dispose any...
steps.instruction: In the fume hood, dispose any waste with SG-II into fluorescence stain waste container (some stain waste has DMSO solvent).

steps.title: RNA standard curve
steps.instruction: RNA standard curve

steps.title: Concentrations of RNA standards in the microplate
steps.instruction: Concentrations of RNA standards in the microplate: Use measured RNA primary concentration instead of 200 ug/mL: %s %s %s %s %s %s

steps.title: Slope of fluorescence in Set A...
steps.instruction: Slope of fluorescence in Set A vs concentration of RNA standard gives m RNA+DNase (~0.024) Slope of fluorescence in Set B vs concentration of RNA standard gives m RNA+RNase

steps.title: Calculate ρ (<=0
steps.instruction: Calculate ρ (<=0.15)

steps.title: Total RNA of the samples Where,...
steps.instruction: Total RNA of the samples Where, RFU A and RFU C are the fluorescence in Tube A and Tube C of the same sample. RFU ABlank and RFU CBlank are the fluorescence in Tube A and Tube C of the blank.

steps.title: DNA standard curve
steps.instruction: DNA standard curve

steps.title: Concentrations of DNA standards in the microplate
steps.instruction: Concentrations of DNA standards in the microplate: Use measured DNA primary concentration instead of 500 ug/mL: %s %s %s %s DNA primary Conc (ug/mL)DNA primary (uL)Tris (uL)Conc. DNA secondary (ug/mL)1595 %s %s 2nd (uL)Tris (uL)STEBWS (uL)Conc (ng/mL)Sample (uL)SG II (uL)Final (ng/mL)R10650250500.00190100D1106402505011.311901010.74D2406102505045.221901042.96D310055025050113.0519010107.4

steps.title: Slope of fluorescence in Set A...
steps.instruction: Slope of fluorescence in Set A vs concentration of DNA standard gives m DNA+DNase Slope of fluorescence in Set B vs concentration of DNA standard gives m DNA+RNase (~0.13)

steps.title: Calculate δ (<=0
steps.instruction: Calculate δ (<=0.15)

steps.title: Total DNA of the samples Where,...
steps.instruction: Total DNA of the samples Where, RFU B and RFU C are the fluorescence in Tube B and Tube C of the same sample RFU BBlank and RFU CBlank are the fluorescence in Tube B and Tube C of the blank.

steps.title: Dilution factor=40 If, Sample is extracted...
steps.instruction: Dilution factor=40 If, Sample is extracted by 1 mL extraction reagent In Set 1, sample is diluted to 100/1000 In Set 3, diluted by Tris and all working solutions to 250/950 In microplate, diluted by SG-II WS to 190/200

steps.title: Add 190 µL 5 mM Tris...
steps.instruction: Add 190 µL 5 mM Tris buffer into DNA primary tube.

steps.title: DNA_primary concentration (μg/ml) = (Abs 260...
steps.instruction: DNA_primary concentration (μg/ml) = (Abs 260 -Abs 320 )x 50 μg/ml x (10mm/0.49 mm) X DF Where, DF=20. The DNA primary concentration should be around 500 ug/mL. If the DNA primary concentration is less than 400 (reading from udrop is less than 0.055) or higher than 600 ug/mL, repeat go to step #53 to go to step #60 . Pay more attention to the solution mixing of primary DNA solution.

steps.title: Prepare DNA tertiary standard solution 1 ug/ml
steps.instruction: Prepare DNA tertiary standard solution 1 ug/ml

steps.title: Before loading the samples, use pipet...
steps.instruction: Before loading the samples, use pipet tip to aspire and dispense multiple times for thorough mix.

steps.title: Read absorbance at 260 and 320 nm
steps.instruction: Read absorbance at 260 and 320 nm.

steps.title: Place all tubes into the shaker/incubator...
steps.instruction: Place all tubes into the shaker/incubator at 37 °C , continuously shaking at 200 RPM for 00:20:00 . Incubation time is critical. Temperature might be disturbed by door open/close. Don't start the timer until temperature returns to 37°C.

steps.title: Prepare RNA secondary standard solution 25 ug/ml
steps.instruction: Prepare RNA secondary standard solution 25 ug/ml

steps.title: Add 210 µL Tris buffer into RNA primary tube
steps.instruction: Add 210 µL Tris buffer into RNA primary tube.

steps.title: Prepare RNA tertiary standard solution 2 ug/ml
steps.instruction: Prepare RNA tertiary standard solution 2 ug/ml

steps.title: Remove the DNA and RNA secondary...
steps.instruction: Remove the DNA and RNA secondary out of the biosafety cabinet.

steps.title: RNA_primary concentration (μg/ml) = (Abs 260...
steps.instruction: RNA_primary concentration (μg/ml) = (Abs 260 -Abs 320 )x 40 μg/ml x (10mm/0.49 mm) X DF Where, DF=8. The DNA primary concentration should be around 200 ug/mL. If the DNA primary concentration is less than 150 (reading from udrop is less than 0.055) or higher than 250 ug/mL, repeat go to step #56 to go to step #60 . Pay more attention to the solution mixing of primary RNA solution.

steps.title: Turn on refrigerated centrifuge and set...
steps.instruction: Turn on refrigerated centrifuge and set the temperature to 4 °C . CENTRIFUGE 5430 R (Eppendorf, MP2231000510)

steps.title: Centrifuge extracted samples 10000 x g, 4 °C, 4 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge extracted samples 10000 x g, 4 °C, 4 minutes

steps.title: Invert the tube and mix thoroughly,...
steps.instruction: Invert the tube and mix thoroughly, then aspire 100 µL of supernatant using the reverse pipetting technique.

steps.title: Dispense this 100 uL into the 2 mL microtube
steps.instruction: Dispense this 100 uL into the 2 mL microtube.

steps.title: Return any remaining extract in the...
steps.instruction: Return any remaining extract in the tip back to the extraction tube.

steps.title: In the biosafety cabinet, prepare 3XN...
steps.instruction: In the biosafety cabinet, prepare 3XN 2 mL RNase free microtubes, N = number of samples.

steps.title: Important technique for accurately preparing standards...
steps.instruction: Important technique for accurately preparing standards and working solutions Be aware of the conical shaped microtubes. The conical shaped bottom often retains a small volume of liquid due to surface tension. This residual liquid can impact the accuracy of measurements or the concentration of the solution. (1) For inverting mix: Gently invert the tube several times to ensure that any residual liquid at the bottom is mixed back into the solution. (2) For pipet mix: Place the pipette tip all the way to the bottom of the conical tube and then aspire/dispense multiple times.

steps.title: Use reverse pipetting
steps.instruction: Use reverse pipetting: Load 4 ul DNA and RNA secondary onto the μdrop plate, in duplicate. µDrop™ Plates (Thermo Scientific, N12391) Varioskan LUX Multimode Microplate Reader (Thermo Fisher, VL0L00D0)

steps.title: In the biosafety cabinet, transfer all...
steps.instruction: In the biosafety cabinet, transfer all extract to their corresponding microtubes.

steps.title: Centrifuge extracted samples 10000 x g, 4 °C, 4 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge extracted samples 10000 x g, 4 °C, 4 minutes

steps.title: In the biosafety cabinet, transfer supernatant...
steps.instruction: In the biosafety cabinet, transfer supernatant as much as possible to their corresponding microtubes, avoid disturbing the debris.

steps.title: Mix DNA secondary standard solution by...
steps.instruction: Mix DNA secondary standard solution by aspiring up and down several times with pipet. Do not vortex!

steps.title: Transfer 40 µL DNA secondary solution...
steps.instruction: Transfer 40 µL DNA secondary solution ( 25 ug/ml ) to DNA tertiary standard tube. Keep On ice .

steps.title: Mix RNA secondary standard solution by...
steps.instruction: Mix RNA secondary standard solution by aspiring up and down several times with pipet. Do not vortex!

steps.title: Transfer 120 µL RNA secondary solution...
steps.instruction: Transfer 120 µL RNA secondary solution ( 25 ug/ml ) to RNA tertiary standard tube and mix. Keep On ice .

steps.title: Label DNase tube with "WS-A", add...
steps.instruction: Label DNase tube with "WS-A", add 2X1000+820 mL 5 mM Tris buffer into the tube.

steps.title: Add 60 µL 900 mM MgCl 2 to WS-A
steps.instruction: Add 60 µL 900 mM MgCl 2 to WS-A

steps.title: Filter microalgae in liquid media onto...
steps.instruction: Filter microalgae in liquid media onto polycarbonate filters, using gentle vacuum pressure (130 mmHg).

steps.title: Rinse filter tunnel with filtered artificial...
steps.instruction: Rinse filter tunnel with filtered artificial seawater (nutrient free) to avoid sample loss.

steps.title: Fold the filter with two tweezers
steps.instruction: Fold the filter with two tweezers: (1) Fold in half along its diameter, creating a semicircular shape; (2) Fold once more in the same direction, resulting in a long strip; (3) Fold once more, halving its length, so that sample is secured.

steps.title: Place folded filter in 2 mL cryogenic vial
steps.instruction: Place folded filter in 2 mL cryogenic vial.

steps.title: Blank is not required in this measurement
steps.instruction: Blank is not required in this measurement.

steps.title: Flash-freeze tubes with liquid nitrogen and store at -80 °C
steps.instruction: Flash-freeze tubes with liquid nitrogen and store at -80 °C

steps.title: Place the aliquot and the remaining...
steps.instruction: Place the aliquot and the remaining extract into two separate boxes, i.e., one for aliquot only, the other one for remaining extract.

steps.title: Load diluted samples to each corresponding...
steps.instruction: Load diluted samples to each corresponding tubes (marked in yellow) in Set A, B and C .

steps.title: Add RNA tertiary standard to tubes...
steps.instruction: Add RNA tertiary standard to tubes (marked in pink) in Set A, B and C .

steps.title: Add DNA tertiary standard to tubes...
steps.instruction: Add DNA tertiary standard to tubes (marked in blue) in Set A, B and C .
